summary: Completed comprehensive testing of Scenario Builder backend APIs and frontend UI. All 19 backend API tests pass. Frontend UI fully functional with NGFS templates display, fork dialog, custom scenarios table, and approval queue.

minor.issue: Fixed: Initial version record not being created on scenario creation due to missing commit after _create_version call

issues: Minor: React hydration warnings in console logs (no functional impact)

critical_code_review_comments: The NGFS data is embedded in ngfs_sync_service.py rather than fetched from external API - this is intentional for reliability
ScenarioImpactService uses sample portfolio holdings - would need integration with actual portfolio data in production
seed_data_creation: Test data created and cleaned up during testing. 6 NGFS templates exist in database from prior NGFS sync.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Scenario Builder feature is fully functional. 6 NGFS Phase V templates are synced. Backend uses PostgreSQL (Supabase) for scenarios and MongoDB for portfolios. API endpoints tested: scenarios CRUD, NGFS sync, templates, fork, approval workflow (submit/approve/reject), publish, versions.
passed_tests: - Stats cards show correct counts (6 templates, 9+ custom, 2 pending, 2 sources)
- NGFS Templates tab displays 6 scenarios with Carbon Price, Temp, GDP Impact
- Fork dialog has name and description inputs
- Fork workflow creates new scenario and switches to Custom tab
- Custom Scenarios table shows all columns (Name, Base, Status, Version, Updated, Actions)
- Approval Queue shows pending scenarios with Approve/Reject buttons
- Sync NGFS Data button is present

rca_of_the_issue.issue: Initial version record not created on scenario creation
rca_of_the_issue.root_cause: In scenario_builder_service.py create_scenario method, the _create_version method was called after db.commit() but _create_version uses flush() instead of commit(), so the version record was never persisted to database
rca_of_the_issue.fix_applied: Added db.commit() after _create_version call to ensure the version record is persisted
